codeReview.summary: The new MCP runner negative-path tests look good and they line up with the upstream failure cases. However, the transport-side parity is still incomplete: the new client-factory hook is only observational, and the streamable-HTTP session id is synthesized from the server name instead of reflecting per-session transport state.

issues.description: `MCPTransportClientFactory` is defined as `Fn(MCPTransportClientConfig)` returning `()`, and both `MCPServerSse::connect()` and `MCPServerStreamableHttp::connect()` merely invoke it for side effects (`server.rs:445` and `server.rs:600`). Upstream `httpx_client_factory` is passed through to `sse_client` / `streamablehttp_client` and is used to construct the real `httpx.AsyncClient` (`reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/mcp/server.py:1253-1255`, `1389-1401`, and `137-148`). As shipped, callers cannot alter the transport client or request pipeline at all, so the feature does not actually implement the promised client-factory behavior.

`generated_session_id()` fabricates `generated-session:{name}` from the server name, so every fresh connection for a given server name reports the same synthetic session id. Upstream streamable-HTTP session ids come from the transport/server and can change when a new session is created or a retry/reconnect swaps transports (`reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-core/test/shims/mcp-server/streamableHttpRetry.test.ts:413`, `933`, `1106`, `1196`). Any caller using `session_id()` to distinguish fresh sessions or invalidate per-session caches will incorrectly treat separate sessions as the same session.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission shared-state docs do not capture the transport/session mismatch between the Python and JS MCP references: Python resumes streamable-HTTP sessions via the `Mcp-Session-Id` header, while JS also exposes a first-class `sessionId` transport option. Without that note, future parity work can easily conflate the two shapes and introduce synthetic abstractions that do not match either upstream precisely.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `.factory/library/parity.md` has no MCP transport/session guidance, while `reference/openai-agents-python/src/agents/mcp/server.py:1599-1616` documents header-based session resumption and `reference/openai-agents-js/packages/agents-core/src/mcp.ts:932` exposes a `sessionId` option.

summary: Fail. The negative-path runner coverage is solid, but the new MCP transport surface still misses real client-factory behavior and reports a name-derived streamable-HTTP session id instead of true per-session transport state.
